description_long: This object is a large, professional-grade reel-to-reel tape recorder, most likely used for audio recording or playback in studios or broadcast environments. The front panel features two prominent metallic reels labeled 'TASCAM', a central tape path, and a control interface with multiple buttons, switches, and analog VU meters. The body is a robust, rectangular black metal housing, with visible screws and ports on the sides. The front face is densely populated with controls, including rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a digital display, indicating advanced audio manipulation capabilities. The reels and tape path are exposed, allowing for manual threading and adjustment of magnetic tape. The overall design is utilitarian and industrial, emphasizing durability and precision.
description: A large, black, professional reel-to-reel tape recorder with dual metallic reels, a control panel full of buttons, switches, and analog meters, and a robust rectangular housing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the solid, rectangular black housing and the protruding reels, with minimal visible controls.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the two large TASCAM reels, tape path, and a detailed control panel with meters, buttons, and switches.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the depth of the housing and the reels extending outward.
description_view_3: Rear or top view showing the plain, flat surface of the housing and the tops of the reels peeking out.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, rotary dial, toggle switch
